avalancheActivity.highlights:
High avalanche danger will persist.
The snowpack will be generally unstable.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Snowfalls are forecasted from low to moderate. The wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. They can be released easily. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground. Moist avalanches are possible. Wooded areas could also be affected by avalanches and present situations of instability.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of the last few days is lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack. Whumpfing sounds and shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack are a clear indication. Stability tests and field observations confirm the unfavourable bonding of the snowpack in moderately steep terrain as well. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs remain prone to triggering.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. Light snowfall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will persist.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

Meticulous route selection is required. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate wind from westerly directions, further wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall from the afternoon over a wide area. As a consequence of the new snow the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a considerable danger of dry avalanches will be encountered over a wide area. This applies in particular above the tree line.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, further wind slabs formed. The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in all aspects and generally above the tree line. This applies in particular on steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanches can be released in the new snow and wind slab layers and reach large size.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended. Backcountry touring calls for great caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the moderate westerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers.

Above approximately 1500 m a lot of snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 800 m snow will fall from the afternoon over a wide area. As a consequence of the new snow the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in some cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the large surface-area wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered now as before.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude. On steep sunny slopes the snowpack will only just freeze.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs of last week represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Over a wide area easily released wind slabs formed. The wind slabs are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar above the tree line. Snow profiles and stability tests have confirmed the unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily. Individual gliding avalanches can also be released in the night or in the morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The deep wind slabs of Friday remain in some cases prone to triggering.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs of Friday represent the main danger. They can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. Mostly they are shallow, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and on steep slopes in particular above the tree line.
On very steep shady slopes the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack. Slopes that have been little used this winter thus far are especially unfavourable.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.
More natural avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Friday: 10 to 30 cm of snow, and up to 40 cm in some localities, has fallen above approximately 1200 m.
The fresh snow and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow and partly also surface hoar. In some cases the wind slabs have bonded poorly with the old snowpack.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a slow strengthening of the snowpack. Little snow will fall until Monday.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in steep terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs represent the main danger. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas and on steep, little used slopes. At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and the danger is greater.
The avalanches are medium-sized and can mostly be released by large loads.
In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be well bonded over a wide area. Field observations and stability tests have shown this situation.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on wind-loaded slopes. The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: No distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 1200 m snow will fall from the afternoon over a wide area. As a consequence of the new snow the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A treacherous avalanche situation will persist. The snowpack remains prone to triggering.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line.

The snowpack will be moist at low altitude. On steep sunny slopes the snowpack will only just freeze.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible below approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: Areas where the snow cover is rather shallow where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. In gullies and bowls and in extremely steep terrain individual small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist slab avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: In the last few days mostly small wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls. In some places new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. This applies especially on steep shady slopes, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. In very isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

The snowpack will be moist below approximately 2200 m.

tendency.highlights: Moderate avalanche danger will prevail. Fresh wind slabs require caution.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: As you climb higher in altitude, danger spots are more widespread.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs represent the main danger.
Winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places. Mostly they are shallow. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep, little used slopes.
In some places avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack. These are medium-sized and can mostly only be released by large loads.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.
Small to medium-sized dry snow slides and avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Friday: 5 to 10 cm of snow fell during the night above approximately 1200 m.
The fresh snow and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow and partly also surface hoar.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: The danger will persist. Little snow will fall until Monday.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. Gliding avalanches can also occur.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
Gliding avalanches can also occur at any time.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
